boundary: Goal1125 is a prioritization audit only. It does not edit public wording, authorize speedup claims, start cloud resources, or release v1.0.

rows.allowed_claim: prepared DB compact-summary traversal/filter/grouping sub-path may enter claim review; no DBMS or SQL-engine speedup claim
rows.current_boundary: Prepared DB compact-summary traversal/filter/grouping is RT-core ready, but no public speedup wording is authorized yet.

goal1060_rejected_rows.reason: RTX is slower than the fastest non-OptiX same-semantics baseline in current final A5000 evidence.
RTX is slower than the fastest non-OptiX same-semantics baseline in current final A5000 evidence.

rows.next_local_action: Profile prepared DB compact-summary transfer and aggregation overhead; reduce Python row materialization before a broader RTX rerun.
rows.why: Both compact-summary RTX rows are slower than Embree compact-summary baselines in Goal1060, so another paid pod run would mostly remeasure known overhead.

rows.allowed_claim: bounded graph visibility any-hit plus native BFS/triangle graph-ray candidate-generation sub-paths may enter claim review; no whole-app graph speedup claim
rows.current_boundary: Only the bounded graph visibility_edges any-hit traversal sub-path is covered; BFS frontier bookkeeping, triangle set-intersection, shortest-path logic, graph database behavior, distributed analytics, Python setup, and whole-app graph speedup remain outside this wording.

goal1060_rejected_rows.reason: RTX is slower than the fastest non-OptiX same-semantics baseline in current final A5000 evidence.

rows.next_local_action: Split RT traversal timing from BFS/triangle bookkeeping and reduce host-side frontier or set-intersection overhead before retesting on RTX.
rows.why: Goal1060 shows the graph visibility RTX gate slower than the Embree graph-ray baseline, and graph bookkeeping phases are not cleanly separated enough for wording.

rows.allowed_claim: native-assisted candidate-discovery path only; no full polygon-area speedup claim
rows.current_boundary: Only native-assisted LSI/PIP candidate discovery is covered; exact polygon-area continuation, row materialization, Python setup, arbitrary polygon geometry, and whole-app polygon-overlap speedup remain outside this wording.

goal1060_rejected_rows.reason: RTX is slower than the fastest non-OptiX same-semantics baseline in current final A5000 evidence.

rows.next_local_action: Fix candidate discovery/chunking and exact-area handoff. Public wording can only cover candidate discovery unless exact-area refinement becomes native.
rows.why: The native-assisted RTX candidate-discovery phase loses badly to the PostGIS same-unit-cell contract in Goal1060.

rows.allowed_claim: native-assisted candidate-discovery path only; no full Jaccard speedup claim
rows.current_boundary: Native-assisted LSI/PIP candidate discovery is RT-core ready, but no public speedup wording is authorized yet.

goal1060_rejected_rows.reason: RTX is slower than the fastest non-OptiX same-semantics baseline in current final A5000 evidence.

rows.next_local_action: Fix Jaccard candidate discovery/chunking and document that exact set-area/Jaccard refinement remains CPU/Python-owned until a native reducer exists.
rows.why: The native-assisted RTX Jaccard candidate phase is slower than the Embree candidate baseline in Goal1060.
